title: Local-First Architecture with Room (SQLite) for MVP
context: The app must work fully offline (BR-003, FR-008). We need a database for the MVP that requires no network, is well-supported on Android, and allows complex aggregate queries for balance computation and reporting. The future requires a migration path to cloud sync.
decision: Use Room (an ORM over SQLite) as the sole data store for the MVP. All reads and writes go directly to the local Room database. Supabase cloud sync is explicitly deferred to post-MVP (T8 in the plan) and will be added via a WorkManager-based SyncWorker without changing the Repository interface.

alternatives.name: Firestore (Firebase)
alternatives.description: NoSQL cloud-first database with offline caching.
pros: Real-time sync out of the box
cons: Complex and costly aggregate queries (no GROUP BY, SUM) — would require heavy client-side processing
Unpredictable cost scaling at thousands of users
Offline mode is a cache, not the primary store — subtle edge cases

alternatives.name: Supabase from Day 1
alternatives.description: Use Supabase as the primary store, with local caching.
pros: SQL aggregations available from day one
cons: Significantly more complex setup before core features are working
Requires auth/family group design before any feature can be built
Offline handling is a non-trivial problem requiring a full sync engine

positive: Zero network dependency for MVP — works anywhere, any time
Room's Flow-based queries provide automatic reactive UI updates
SQL schema for complex balance/reporting queries without client-side aggregation
Repository pattern already abstracts storage — adding Supabase sync later requires no ViewModel/UseCase changes
negative: Data lives only on one device until Supabase sync is built
Multi-device family sharing is not possible during MVP phase
neutral: Schema migrations will be needed when Supabase schema is introduced
